body:
## Issue #649 â€” Symbolic world models & contexts

Closes #649. Implements the world-model feature the issue describes, using
**links networks (not embeddings)** as the meaning representation and the
**relative-meta-logic** kernel as the dependent-logic layer.

### What this PR delivers

- **`src/world_model.rs`** â€” the core implementation:
  - **`Context`** â€” a first-class *links network* (`SubstitutionGraph`) plus a
    set of **dependent statements**. Each context is always a links network; the
    statement layer mirrors into the graph and serializes via
    `Context::links_notation`.
  - **`WorldModel`** â€” holds the per-dialogue **current** state, the **target**
    state the user wants, and a shared **general** context.
  - **`Action`** â€” a STRIPS-style add/delete set of link edits, content-addressed
    via `stable_id`.
  - **`Context::difference` / `WorldModel::difference`** â€” expose the
    currentâ†’target delta as `to_add` / `to_remove` / `conflicting` links
    (requirement: "expose the difference between current and target").
  - **`Context::predict`** â€” predict the consequences of an action by applying it
    to a **clone**, recalculating, and diffing; the real model is never mutated
    (requirement: "predict the consequences of an action").
  - **`Context::recalculate`** â€” a bounded **JTMS-style cascade** that re-fires
    `StatementAssessment` for every dependent statement to a fixpoint on the RML
    decimal grid, so any change recalculates all statement probabilities;
    provably terminates even on negative dependency cycles.
  - **`Context::merge_from` / `split_off`** and
    **`WorldModel::commit_current_to_general`** â€” ATMS-style **merge/split** of
    context models.
- **`tests/unit/issue_649_world_model.rs`** â€” 10 tests covering the links-network
  invariant, the difference/target-reached logic, dependent-statement fixpoint
  recalculation, negative-cycle termination, predict-does-not-mutate, statement
  movement in a prediction, and merge/split/commit.
- **Design case study** under `docs/case-studies/issue-649/` â€” deep analysis with
  cited online research (STRIPS/PDDL, situation calculus, JTMS/ATMS, AGM belief
  revision, JEPA world models), a per-requirement solution plan and prior-art
  survey, the full requirement list (R649-01 â€¦ R649-19), the conceptâ†’associative-
  stack mapping, requirement rows **R428â€“R434** in `REQUIREMENTS.md`, and the
  `tests/unit/docs_requirements_issue_649.rs` traceability test.

### Reused, not reinvented

The implementation builds on existing substrate: `SubstitutionGraph` for the
links network, the `relative_meta_logic` kernel (`TruthValue`, `Aggregator`,
`StatementAssessment`) for dependent probabilities, and `stable_id` for
content-addressed IDs. No embeddings or neural components â€” consistent with
`NON-GOALS.md`.

### Requirement status

Of the 14 conceptual requirements: **9 realized** (7 of them via the new
`world_model` module), 4 partial (pipeline-integration steps: seed current from
the append-only log, build target from `IntentFormalization`, route "I wantâ€¦"
into target edits, render via `self_explanation`), 1 proposed (the agentâ‡„user
target-synchronization loop). See
[`docs/case-studies/issue-649/requirements.md`](docs/case-studies/issue-649/requirements.md).

### Verification

- `cargo fmt --check` â€” clean
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (pedantic + nursery + `-D warnings`,
  `unsafe_code = "forbid"`) â€” 0 warnings
- `cargo test` â€” **1519 passed, 0 failed** (incl. the 10 new world-model tests and
  the traceability test)
- `main` merged into the branch.
title: Issue #649: symbolic world models & contexts (implementation + case study)
